[154:03]There have been interventionists, and those have been people like John McCain and Lindsey

[154:08]Graham and George W. Bush, and there have been isolationists, and then the most prominent

[154:13]of those have been Ron Paul and Rand Paul and their others, and people perceive those

[154:17]are the two choices.

[154:18]You've got to be one or the other.

[154:20]I've always thought both were wrong.

[154:24]Um, the way I view my own foreign policy...

[154:26]I'm with you, by the way, for whatever it's worth.

[154:28]I agree with you.

[154:29]Okay, good.

[154:30]I don't know who set up that binary, but there are lots of choices, actually.

[154:33]I mean, people sort of naturally fall into...

[154:36]I think they want to classify people, and they're like,

[154:38]okay, you're one or the other, and you got to be all or nothing.

[154:41]And the interventionist, it seems,

[154:44]have never seen a country they didn't want to invade,

[154:46]and that doesn't make any sense to me.

[154:47]And the isolationist, I think, don't take the threats to America seriously and I think that's naive and it doesn't work.

[155:09]I mean the central touch point.

[155:12]for U.S. foreign policy and for any question a military intervention.

[155:51]around the world. We have troops deployed in many countries around the world. We protect

[289:42]I didn't get into where we disagree on policy on that speech. I just said, you know, the man is an American hero, and I'm proud to serve with him.

[289:47]But that was meant to be a statement also, that if you attack me, I'm going to praise you, not for things that are not praiseworthy.

[289:52]If I disagree with you, I will not be shy about saying it, but for things that are praiseworthy.
